title: Notion API Integration for Metrics Export
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to push my metrics dictionary to a Notion database so that I can maintain a synchronized copy of my metrics in Notion for collaboration and documentation purposes.
description:
Integrate DataTools Pro with the Notion API to allow users to export their metrics dictionary into a Notion database. This is the first third-party data publishing integration, following the same pattern used for internal API calls and AI analyst services.

The integration flow:
1. User enters their Notion integration secret key (stored encrypted in the database)
2. Application fetches available Notion pages shared with that integration
3. User selects a target page and provides a database name
4. Application creates a new database under the selected page and pushes all metrics

Initially only metric name and definition were pushed. Later enhanced (MBT-861) to include Line of Business, Topic, and Business Owner columns.

For production use, the integration supports OAuth (client ID + client secret) for a more seamless authorization experience.
acceptance_criteria: User can enter their Notion integration secret key on a settings page
Secret key is stored encrypted in the database
Application fetches and displays Notion pages shared with the integration
User can select a target page and specify a database name
Clicking 'Start Pushing' creates a new database and pushes all metrics
Metrics include name and definition columns (later enhanced with more fields)
Error handling for invalid keys, missing pages, and API failures
User can update their integration secret key at any time
ux_changes: New Notion integration modal accessible from the metrics page. Includes a form for entering/editing the Notion secret key, a page selector dropdown, database name input, and a 'Start Pushing' action button. Edit secret option available in the top-right corner for returning users.
middleware_changes: Notion API client that handles authentication, page listing (via POST /search), database creation, and row insertion. Handles pagination for large page lists and validates integration permissions.
data_model_changes: New table to store encrypted Notion integration credentials (secret key, optionally client ID and client secret for OAuth) per user/team.
api_changes: Outbound calls to Notion API: POST /v1/search (list pages), POST /v1/databases (create database), POST /v1/pages (insert rows). OAuth flow via /v1/oauth/authorize for production access.
implementation_notes: Notion API only returns pages explicitly shared with the integration. Users must share pages with their Notion integration before they appear in the picker. The OAuth client credentials were set up for production use with a redirect URI to app.datatoolspro.com/notion.
comment_summary: Extended implementation cycle with iterative refinement. Developer built the encrypted secret storage, page picker, and database creation flow. Key discovery: Notion pages must be explicitly shared with the integration to appear via API. OAuth flow was configured for production. This ticket was eventually marked as a duplicate of the broader Notion OAuth integration work.
